name: Jamaica

visa_free.notes: Visa-free; Commonwealth country with 6-month unrestricted stay
Visa-free; Commonwealth country with 6-month unrestricted stay
Visa-free; Commonwealth country with 6-month unrestricted stay
Visa-free; Commonwealth country with 6-month unrestricted stay

Visa-free as a Commonwealth member nation. PICA official table lists 'INDIA (Commonwealth) – NO visa required' with no explicit day cap stated; maximum stay is determined by immigration officer at port of entry (typically up to 6 months for Commonwealth visitors).

Most Commonwealth citizens do not require entry visas; maximum stay of 6 months initially, extendable up to 12 months. Exceptions (require visas): Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Sierra Leone, Cameroon, Cayman Islands (BRIT), and Nigeria. Commonwealth visitors must hold valid passport (min 6 months validity), be in good health, and carry return/onward tickets.
Visa-free for 30 days (Hong Kong SAR passport holders)
Visa-free for 90 days per year under the Jamaica-Russia Bilateral Visa Waiver Agreement signed September 27, 2018. Covers tourism, cultural exchange, and business purposes.
Visa-free; Commonwealth country with 6-month unrestricted stay

CARICOM member state nationals generally receive visa-free access of up to 6 months under the CARICOM Single Market and Economy (CSME) free movement of skilled persons framework. Jamaica is a CARICOM member state.

visa_policy.notes: Jamaica does not offer an e-visa system. Visas are issued at Jamaican consular posts abroad or, for eligible nationalities, at ports of entry. The official country listing PDF (mfaft.gov.jm) is the primary reference document but was not machine-readable. Data for visa_free and visa_on_arrival lists was obtained from the Jamaica Embassy Brussels official website (jamaica-brussels.be) which published a detailed country breakdown, and the Jamaica Embassy Washington DC official site for Commonwealth citizen rules. Visa-required nationalities include: Afghanistan, Algeria, Angola, Bahrain, Benin, Bhutan, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cambodia, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Cayman Islands, Central African Republic, Chad, Comoros, Congo, Cote d'Ivoire, Cuba, Djibouti, Egypt, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Gabon, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Haiti, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Laos, Lebanon, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Mali, Marshall Islands, Mauritania, Micronesia, Mongolia, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Nicaragua, Niger, Nigeria, Oman, Pakistan, Palau, Philippines, Qatar, Rwanda, São Tomé & Príncipe, Saudi Arabia, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Sudan, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Syria, Thailand, Togo, Tunisia, UAE, Vietnam, Yemen, Zimbabwe. Yugoslavia was removed from visa_on_arrival as Yugoslavia is a dissolved state (no longer exists).

conditional_access.conditions: Dominican Republic nationals who hold a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa may enter Jamaica without a visa for up to 30 days. Source table footnote: 'DOMINICAN REPUBLIC – NONE – 30 days (with valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa)'.

Nationals of these 15 countries may enter Jamaica visa-free for 30 days ONLY IF in possession of a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa AND proof of vaccination against Measles, Rubella & Polio. Source footnote verbatim: '*Applicable only if in possession of a valid US, UK, Canadian or Schengen visa AND proof of vaccination against Measles, Rubella & Polio'. Countries marked with * in table: Albania, Belarus, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Estonia, Kazakhstan, Latvia, Lithuania, Macedonia, Moldova, Montenegro, Romania, Serbia, Slovenia.

Nationals of any country (except Taiwan) who hold a valid United States Permanent Resident Card (Green Card / Alien Registration Card) do not require a visa to visit Jamaica, provided travel is being done directly between the USA and Jamaica. Must travel with a valid passport or travel document for the duration of stay. Taiwan nationals are explicitly excluded. Source verbatim: 'applicants who possess a valid United States Permanent Resident Card (Green Card), regardless of nationality do not require a visa to visit Jamaica, if travel is being done directly between the USA and Jamaica.'

Cuban nationals holding Diplomatic & Official passports are exempt from visa requirements for Jamaica. Ordinary Cuban passport holders require a visa (prior to arrival). Source table verbatim: 'CUBA – YES – pta-NO – Diplomatic & Official Passports'.

Haitian nationals holding Diplomatic and Official passports are exempt from visa requirements for Jamaica. Ordinary Haitian passport holders require a visa (prior to arrival). Source table verbatim: 'HAITI – YES – ptaNONE – Diplomatic and Official passports'.

Under the Jamaica-UAE Visa Waiver Agreement announced September 23, 2019, holders of UAE diplomatic and official passports will not need visas to enter Jamaica. Ordinary UAE passport holders require a visa (prior to arrival). The agreement was announced during the first UAE-Caribbean Forum.

cbi.notes: Jamaica explicitly does not offer a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) programme. The Jamaican government has stated that 'citizenship is not for sale.' An Economic Residency Programme was approved in concept in 2021 to offer permanent residency (not citizenship) for significant investment, but as of 2026 this has not been officially launched. Dual citizenship is recognised by Jamaica (confirmed via embassyofjamaica.org). Standard citizenship pathways include: descent, registration (Commonwealth citizens), naturalisation (5+ years residency), and marriage to a Jamaican national. Sources: pica.gov.jm/citizenship, jis.gov.jm/information/returning-residents/jamaican-citizenship/, embassyofjamaica.org/visitors/jamaican_residency.htm

programs.name: Permanent Residence – Foreign Investor Category
programs.notes: Jamaica offers permanent residence to foreign investors; the official PICA page and official consular sources confirm the investor category exists but do not state a specific minimum investment amount on the publicly available pages. The Jamaica Embassy Brussels site lists 'foreign investors in Jamaica' as an eligible category. Processing: minimum 6 months; applicants receive an initial 3-month entry visa, renewable annually for 3 years, after which a residence visa is issued. After 5 years of residency, naturalization to citizenship is possible. A work permit is still required separately. Sources: pica.gov.jm/immigration/permanent-residence, jamaica-brussels.be/permanent-residence/, embassyofjamaica.org/visitors/jamaican_residency.htm

programs.name: Permanent Residence – Retirement Category
programs.notes: Foreign retirees who have resided in Jamaica for over 3 years may apply for permanent residence. Evidence of pension or financial means required. Processing minimum 6 months. Source: jamaica-brussels.be/permanent-residence/ (official Embassy of Jamaica, Belgium site)

programs.name: Permanent Residence – Employment Category
programs.notes: Foreign nationals with a minimum of 5 years of unbroken employment in Jamaica may apply for permanent residence. Source: jamaica-brussels.be/permanent-residence/

programs.name: Work Permit – Special Skills Category
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals offered employment in Jamaica by a Jamaican employer who can demonstrate a genuine need for foreign skills; administered by Ministry of Labour and Social Security
programs.notes: Jamaica's Ministry of Labour and Social Security administers work permits including a Special Skills Work Permit category. Not a standalone fast-track visa but the primary pathway for skilled foreign workers. Source: mlss.gov.jm/departments/work-permit/

agreements: CARICOM (Caribbean Community) – free movement of skilled persons under CSME; Jamaica is a member state and participates in the skilled worker free movement regime
Jamaica-Russia Bilateral Visa Waiver Agreement – signed September 27, 2018; 90 days per year for tourism, cultural exchange, and business (ordinary passports)

validation_notes: ADDED Russia (RUS) to visa_free with 90 days — was completely missing from previous record despite the bilateral Jamaica-Russia Visa Waiver Agreement (signed September 27, 2018) being documented on MFAFT official site.

CORRECTED max_stay_days for Mexico from null to 90 days (per jamaica-brussels.be official table).
CORRECTED max_stay_days for Korea (South) from null to 90 days (per jamaica-brussels.be official table).

CORRECTED max_stay_days for Denmark, Finland, Germany, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Spain from null to 90 days each (per jamaica-brussels.be official table).

CORRECTED max_stay_days for France, Greece, Hungary, Poland, Portugal, Czech Republic, Japan, China, Colombia, Ukraine, Uruguay, Venezuela, Monaco, Argentina, Bolivia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Hong Kong from null to 30 days each (per jamaica-brussels.be official table).

CORRECTED Belarus, Estonia, Kazakhstan, Latvia, Lithuania, Romania, Serbia, Montenegro, Slovenia entries in visa_free: these countries carry a '*' footnote in the official table indicating conditional access only (requires valid US/UK/Canadian/Schengen visa + vaccination proof). Removed them from unconditional visa_free; they are correctly captured only in conditional_access.

REMOVED Yugoslavia from visa_on_arrival — Yugoslavia is a dissolved state (ceased to exist 1992/2003) and does not correspond to any current ISO country. Entry added note to this effect in visa_policy notes.
ADDED Taiwan note about Affidavit of Identity requirement (needed because Jamaica does not recognise Taiwan as a state).
CORRECTED Singapore max_stay_days from null to 180 (Commonwealth country, 6-month unrestricted stay, confirmed by jamaica-brussels.be 'unrestricted' group).
ADDED Jamaica-Russia Bilateral Visa Waiver Agreement to agreements array.
No changes to conditional_access entries for Dominican Republic, Honduras, the 15-country Eastern European/Central Asian group, US Green Card holders, Cuba, Haiti, or UAE — all confirmed accurate.

ADDED India (IND) as an explicit visa_free entry — PICA official table (pica.gov.jm/immigration/entry-visa-requirements, last updated July 19 2023) lists 'INDIA (Commonwealth) – NO visa required'; aggregator claim of visa-free is CONFIRMED correct; India was previously only covered by the generic Commonwealth entry with no individual record.
gaps: The PICA entry-visa-requirements page (pica.gov.jm/immigration/entry-visa-requirements) was successfully machine-read via Firecrawl stealth proxy (June 2026); it contains the full A-Z country table last updated July 19, 2023.
The MFAFT 2023 Country Listing PDF (mfaft.gov.jm) was not machine-readable (binary/image PDF); full authoritative per-country visa category list unavailable from that source.
The Jamaica Embassy Washington DC visa_country_listing.pdf was also a non-readable binary PDF.

Specific minimum investment amounts for the Foreign Investor Permanent Residence category are not stated on currently accessible official pages (PICA 403 error); values cited in non-official sources (e.g., USD 250,000 real estate) could not be verified on an official government page and are omitted.

The visa_on_arrival list (Andorra, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Taiwan, Turkmenistan) is sourced from the Jamaica Embassy Brussels website which is official but may not be fully up to date.
Exact max_stay_days for visa_on_arrival nationalities are unconfirmed — not stated on any accessible official page.
No e-visa system exists; enterjamaica.gov.jm is an electronic C5 customs/immigration declaration form only, not an e-visa.
No digital nomad visa or startup visa program found on any official Jamaican government source.
The proposed Economic Residency Programme (2021 announcement) has not been officially launched as of the available sources; no official program page exists.

OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder status: no mention found on any official Jamaican immigration source; OCI cardholders entering Jamaica appear to be treated by their passport nationality only.
Canada permanent resident card as standalone conditional access credential: not referenced in any official Jamaican immigration source checked; only US Green Card is explicitly mentioned.
UK Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) / settlement as standalone credential: not referenced in any official Jamaican immigration source checked.
UAE-Jamaica agreement (announced September 23, 2019): the MFAFT announcement does not specify a stay duration for visa-exempt diplomatic/official UAE passport holders.

A full list of all bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport visa waiver agreements for Jamaica was not available in machine-readable form from any official source; only Cuba, Haiti, and UAE were identified with certainty.

The Jamaica-Russia Visa Waiver Agreement (2018) does not specify which passport types are covered in the MFAFT press release; assumed to cover ordinary passports based on context (reciprocal agreement for tourism/business/cultural exchange).

Sierra Leone appears as 'unrestricted' in the jamaica-brussels.be table but is listed as a Commonwealth country that REQUIRES a visa on the embassyofjamaica.org/visitors/commonwealth_citizens.htm page. The Commonwealth-specific page is more authoritative for Commonwealth country exceptions; Sierra Leone is therefore treated as visa-required.

outbound_official.notes: Source: PICA (Passport, Immigration and Citizenship Agency) Visa Unit — 'Visa Requirements for Jamaicans Travelling Overseas', last updated July 19, 2023. Hosted on the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Foreign Trade (MFAFT) website. The document lists each country with 'NONE' (no visa required), 'YES – pta' (visa required prior to arrival), 'YES – poe' (visa on arrival), or 'YES – E-visa' for Jamaican ordinary passport holders. Conditional entries (e.g. Costa Rica, Cyprus, Guatemala, Honduras which require a third-country visa to enter without a prior visa) are excluded from visa_free as they involve a prerequisite credential. Netherlands Antilles territories (Aruba, Bonaire, Curacao, Sint Maarten, Sint Eustatius, Saba) also require a US/Canadian/Schengen visa as a prerequisite, so excluded from unconditional visa_free. Dominican Republic entry requires tourist card purchase on arrival (treated as visa_free). Venezuela ordinary PP holders receive a tourist card on the flight (treated as visa_free). Russia is listed as YES – pta for Jamaicans (visa required); this is separate from Russians visiting Jamaica (visa-free per bilateral agreement). The PDF also covers Macau (listed as 'See China' — YES for Jamaicans) and Guadeloupe, Martinique, St. Martin as French territories (YES – pta, Schengen visa required).
